title: Diagnostic
description: One JSONL line emitted by cargo-evidence in `--format=jsonl` mode. This is a WIRE-FORMAT schema for streamed stdout, not a committed bundle file; `cargo evidence schema show diagnostic` prints it, but `cargo evidence schema validate` deliberately does not recognize diagnostic files by filename. The ten Schema Rules — exit codes, stdout/stderr split, locked codes, flush semantics, --json alias permanence, FixHint forward-compat, cascading independence, Location precedence, schema-show wiring, Severity closed — live in the `description` fields below.

code.description: Stable UPPER_SNAKE_CASE identifier, domain-prefixed (VERIFY_*, BUNDLE_*, TRACE_*, BOUNDARY_*, GIT_*, HASH_*, SCHEMA_*, POLICY_*, ENV_*, SIGN_*, DIFF_*, INFO_*). Schema Rule 1: a `code` ending in `_OK` is a successful terminal event (exit 0); a `code` ending in `_FAIL` is a failure terminal event (exit 2). Agents key on this field, never on `message`. Schema Rule 3: codes are locked by `diagnostic_codes_locked` contract test (uniqueness + this regex).

severity.description: Schema Rule 10: closed enum. Unknown values fail deserialization — opposite policy from `fix_hint.kind` because severity drives exit-code decisions. Adding a variant is a schema-version bump.

message.description: Human-readable one-liner. Stable across patch releases but not a match target. Agents SHOULD key on `code`; humans see this in logs.

location.description: Schema Rule 8: when both `toml_path` and `entry_uid` are present, agents MUST prefer `toml_path` (structural, stable across renames) over `entry_uid` (semantic UUID). `file`/`line`/`col` are the last fallback for non-TOML sources. Any combination of fields may be omitted.

toml_path.description: Structural locator, JSON-pointer style (e.g. `requirements[2].uid`). Agent-preferred target for autofix.

entry_uid.description: Semantic UUID fallback. Stable across renames of the human-readable `id`.

fix_hint.description: Schema Rule 6: forward-compatible via `#[serde(other)]`. Agents MUST tolerate unknown `kind` values — future versions will add variants; consumers written against this schema MUST NOT panic or reject diagnostics carrying unknown hint kinds. Treat `kind: other` (and any unknown kind) as 'a hint exists but take no automated action'.

kind.description: Discriminant for the hint variant. Known values in this schema version: `assign_uuid`, `add_toml_key`, `other`. Agents encountering an unknown `kind` MUST treat the hint as opaque.

path.description: File to edit (for variants that apply to a file).

toml_path.description: Structural locator for the target entry or table.

value_stub.description: Placeholder value the human should replace (for `add_toml_key`).

x-exit-code-contract.description: Schema Rule 1 — exit-code ↔ terminal-event mapping. The last JSONL line on stdout carries the terminal event; the process exit code matches it.

mapping.0: last JSONL line's `code` ends in `_OK` (happy path)
mapping.1: runtime error (I/O, parse, config); NO terminal event emitted on stdout; human-readable error text goes to stderr
mapping.2: last JSONL line's `code` ends in `_FAIL` (verification or validation failed)

x-stream-semantics.description: Schema Rule 2 — stdout/stderr split. In `--format=jsonl` mode, stdout contains ONLY JSONL (one JSON object per line, no prose, no banners, no null bytes). Human progress text, `tracing::*` output, and warnings continue to go to stderr, unchanged. Schema Rule 4 — flush per event: the CLI flushes stdout after every line so streaming agents get sub-pipe-buffer latency.

x-cascading-semantics.description: Schema Rule 7 — events are independent observations. A single root cause MAY produce multiple downstream diagnostic events (e.g., a corrupted `SHA256SUMS` triggers one `VERIFY_HASH_MISMATCH` per file). Agents SHOULD deduplicate by severity-ranked code before proposing fixes; they MUST NOT assume each diagnostic is an independent root cause.

x-json-alias.description: Schema Rule 5 — `--json` is a permanent synonym for `--format=json`. Both emit the single-terminal-object shape (pre-existing contract, not this schema). `--format=jsonl` is the streaming shape described in this file. No deprecation is planned for `--json`.
